data.body:
WS3 approach decided via research (Joe's directive — what actively-maintained projects do): OPTION 1 — encapsulate chunk+merge INSIDE the verify step, the mainstream LLM-framework pattern (LangChain MapReduceDocumentsChain, LlamaIndex map_reduce/tree_summarize all hide chunking inside the operation; LangGraph Send+reducer and Dagster .collect()/Beam Combine are the heavier workflow-level fan-in primitives — gold standard for general engines but more machinery). Consensus: with 1M-token windows chunking is rarely triggered, so the COMMON case stays ONE aggregate call (no behavior change); chunking is a rare safety valve.
Concrete design for our engine: (1) a sizing helper computes global-index-preserving, token-budgeted chunks of the findings (est_tokens(system+instructions)+per-finding output reserve vs floor(largest_window_tokens(model)*headroom)); (2) verify_inputs emits instruction_chunks (a LIST; 1 element in the common case); (3) RunnerAgentStep gains GENERIC support: when a prompt step's instructions input is a list, it runs the prompt once per chunk and MERGES the structured output by concatenating list-valued fields (verifications) — encapsulated in the step, like LangChain. Single-oversized finding that can't fit even the top model's window is simply NOT verified → pass3_decide(None) already routes it to INDETERMINATE (free, no special path). Refs: LangChain MapReduceDocumentsChain; LlamaIndex response synthesizers (map_reduce, tree_summarize); LangGraph Send API + Annotated[list, operator.add] reducer; Dagster .collect(); AWS Step Functions Map array-aggregation.
